explanations.5: This shows the database connection details: it's PostgreSQL running on IP 1.2.3.4, listening on port 5438 (a non-standard port), with the database name 'amazing'.
explanations.12: This 'Host' keyword defines a named SSH configuration block—when you type 'ssh MyAmazingApp_PROD', it will use the settings defined in the lines below it.
explanations.20: Notice the database name is 'amazing_newx' (different from production's 'amazing'), so the test environment has its own separate database.
explanations.26: This creates another SSH host configuration block, this time for the test environment with a different IP address and username.
